edges.description: Without the activator, galactosylceramide cannot be degraded and accumulates.
edges.source: Saposin A Deficiency from PSAP Variants

edges.description: Substrate accumulation activates autophagy as a secondary cascade.

edges.target: Saposin A Deficiency from PSAP Variants

nodes.description: Galactosylceramide accumulation in patient fibroblasts is accompanied by activation of autophagy, a secondary autophagic-lysosomal cascade documented for the first time in human saposin A deficiency.

evidence.snippet: Fibroblast studies showed GalCer accumulation and the activation of autophagy for the first time in a case of human saposin A deficiency

nodes.description: Undegraded galactosylceramide accumulates and, with cytotoxic psychosine, drives progressive central and peripheral demyelination (globoid cell leukodystrophy).

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the saposin A coding region of the prosaposin gene in an infant presenting as Krabbe disease: first report of saposin A deficiency in humans.
evidence.snippet:
sulphatide (stearoyl-1-14C) uptake indicated an abnormal storage of
galactosylceramide

meta.association: Biallelic PSAP variants affecting the saposin A domain

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the saposin A coding region of the prosaposin gene in an infant presenting as Krabbe disease: first report of saposin A deficiency in humans.
evidence.snippet: A three base pair deletion was found in the saposin A coding sequence of the prosaposin gene

nodes.description: Saposin A arises from proteolytic cleavage of prosaposin (encoded by PSAP). Biallelic PSAP variants affecting the saposin A domain abolish saposin A, the in vivo activator that galactosylceramidase requires to degrade galactosylceramide. The GALC gene and enzyme are intact; the defect is a missing activator cofactor.
nodes.id: Saposin A Deficiency from PSAP Variants

evidence.reference_title: The Second Case of Saposin A Deficiency and Altered Autophagy.
evidence.snippet: Both the enzyme galactosylceramidase and its in vivo activator molecule, saposin A, are essential during GalCer degradation.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the saposin A coding region of the prosaposin gene in an infant presenting as Krabbe disease: first report of saposin A deficiency in humans.
evidence.snippet: A three base pair deletion was found in the saposin A coding sequence of the prosaposin gene
